frames: The agent begins in an upright posture, then the lower segment starts to bend forward, causing a slight lean.
The lower segment continues to bend, and the entire structure leans further forward, losing its initial balance.
The agent's body is significantly angled, with the lower segment nearly parallel to the ground, indicating a loss of upright stability.
The agent is actively falling forward, with the upper segment now angled sharply downwards towards the ground.
The agent's lower segment begins to lift off the ground as it rotates, with the upper segment now pointing almost horizontally.
The rotation continues, bringing the lower segment upwards and the upper segment downwards, inverting its orientation.
The agent completes its rotation, with the lower segment now pointing upwards and the upper segment pointing downwards.
The agent stabilizes in an inverted upright posture, balancing on its "head" with the lower segment extended vertically.
